description_long: This house features a steeply pitched, multi-gabled roof with brown shingles and a complex, asymmetrical silhouette. The front facade includes a covered porch with steps leading up to the main entry, supported by posts and partially enclosed by a railing. The exterior walls are primarily gray, with minimal decorative trim and a mix of window sizes, some set into dormers. The roofline is irregular, with one section appearing damaged or incomplete, exposing part of the attic or upper story. Windows are distributed asymmetrically, and the house has a prominent front-facing gable with decorative woodwork. The overall design suggests a blend of Victorian and Craftsman influences, with an emphasis on verticality and intricate roof geometry.
description: A gray house with a steep, multi-gabled brown shingle roof, asymmetrical windows, and a covered front porch with steps. The design features complex rooflines and decorative woodwork, blending Victorian and Craftsman styles.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry porch, steps, and prominent front-facing gable with decorative trim.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the steep roof pitch, overhanging eaves, and asymmetrical window placement.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the irregular roofline, a dormer, and a damaged or incomplete roof section.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the vertical gable, minimal windows, and extended roof overhang.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with steps, posts, and decorative gable trim
